title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Contract Lens: Http1 Http2 Http3 Boundary
summary: A Node server inherits defaults without understanding header, request, keep-alive, upgrade, idle-socket, HTTP/2, or graceful-close behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts http1 http2 http3 boundary increment in Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Http1 Http2 Http3 Boundary · Theory Http1 Http2 Http3 Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8174 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
